title: Change in semantics for 'podman run --pull'?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Prior to podman-2.0.x (possibly as recently as release 2.0.1?) the '`--pull`' argument to `podman run` did not need a parameter.

Now, however, the parameter appears mandatory, with the `--pull` option consuming the following argument (e.g. `podman run --pull --rm ...` results in:

```
Error: invalid pull policy "--rm"
```

Whilst the `--help` text states:

```
Pull image before creating ("always"|"missing"|"never") (default "missing")
```

The fact that a default is mentioned suggests that the `--pull` option is intended to be allowed to be used without a policy, in which case it inherits the default setting?

This appears to have recently been changed - was this the intention?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.2
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.2
Git Commit:   201c9505b88f451ca877d29a73ed0f1836bb96c7
Built:        Wed Jul  8 17:39:51 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
This might have changed from podman 1.9 to podman 2.0, but this is a CLI issue, I believe, and I am not sure I can fix it,  The idea of defaults, is that if you don't specify the option then it will take the default. If you specify --pull then it expects a flag.
@baude @TomSweeneyRedHat Any thoughts on this?  Is there a way we could ignore the fact that there is no value on --pull?

@srcshelton thanks for the issue.  I'm not sure if this is an unintentional change, I know there were some discussions about `--pull` at some point.  However, we either need to allow pull to take no values and/or update the man page because that's clearly wrong now.  @baude, do you remember if this was intentionally changed?
The manpages were updated with it (`--pull=always|missing|never` is in the `podman run` manpage) so it looks intentional?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
